title: Ceropegia keithii
taxonomy: Ceropegia keithii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This family is commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Within the Apocynaceae family, Ceropegia keithii is further classified as part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which includes the milkweeds and other related species. The genus Ceropegia is a group of twining plants native to Africa, Asia, and Australia. Ceropegia keithii specifically is native to India.

intro: Ceropegia keithii, also known as the "Rat Tail Cactus," is a succulent plant species native to India, characterized by its unique, whip-like flowers and tubular stems. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as antioxidant and wound-healing effects. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia keithii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and digestive issues.
